question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the regulatory intensity across Mutual Funds, PMS, and AIFs?

answer.text: All three are equally regulated by SEBI
Mutual funds have the most stringent regulation (high-touch) while PMS and AIFs have lighter regulation (light-touch)
AIFs are most stringently regulated as they manage the largest amounts
PMS has the lightest regulation as it serves only institutional investors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Mutual funds cater to retail investors and are subjected to high-touch regulation. PMS and AIFs have light-touch regulation because they serve institutional and HNI investors who are expected to understand complex strategies and risks. The investment restrictions on PMS and AIFs are also relatively less. **The Trap:** More money doesn't mean more regulation — the regulatory intensity is based on investor sophistication, not fund size. **Cross-Exam Context:** Regulatory comparison across managed portfolio types is tested in X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** AIFs and PMS are often called 'rich man's mutual funds' — the lower regulation means investors need to conduct more due diligence. As an adviser, your role in PMS/AIF selection is more critical than for mutual funds.
